transcript: Thanks guys. So I'm I'm here to talk about my new book which came out yesterday called Solana Rising. And so uh just want to spend a few minutes on the book because I think it's very important for people here uh who are Solana believers to convince the non-believers about Solana and that was the purpose of writing the book. Uh we spent time interviewing Anatoly and Raj and Lily and a whole host of the founding group of Solana people and the Solana family. And I'm probably the only person in this room that's wearing a tie other than the security people, okay? So I'm probably also one of the oldest people in the room. So I want you to pay attention to this message because the people in my demography, I'm about to turn 62 years old, uh they don't understand it. They don't understand crypto, they don't understand the blockchain, they also don't understand Solana, and I thought it was very important to lay out the story because I think 2026 is going to be one of these breakthrough years for this chain. Uh couple of predictions for you. The Clarity Act, which I think is very likely even though there's some Democratic opposition right now, I think it's very likely that it's going to be passed next year uh and this is going to create this wonderful pathway for tokenization of real world assets. And so we could be sitting at the end of 2026, we could be at a Solana Breakpoint next year where you're seeing large scale companies like BlackRock, Blackstone, JPMorgan, and a whole host of traditional finance people using this network uh to transact. And in the process of doing the research for the book, uh we estimate that there's about four trillion dollars of transactions globally that are spent in... I'm sorry, there's four billion... trillion dollars of costs spent on transaction verification. And so that would include credit card fees, wire fees, selling your house, paying the attorneys, the accounting fees, etc. And so I just want you to think about this global opportunity if we can move things into this great operating layer uh and reduce those transaction costs. How much innovation... how much inno... innovation economically, uh but also here for the people that are in this room. So uh for me, uh when I travel the world and I talk to people about Solana, uh they're not the people in this room. Uh they're people who are older, don't understand it, don't like it, and you can feel that institutional resistance and so I think it's very important for all of us here to be proselytizers of of this great vision that Anatoly and Raj have put together. And so the title of the book is uh Solana Rising. I appreciate you guys giving me the opportunity to speak about it. Uh it's on Amazon. I like to tease people and say it's an international bestseller and if you don't believe me you can come into my basement, I'll show you every copy I bought to make it that way. Uh but I hope you guys will buy the book uh and pass it on to your friends uh and I think 2026 is going to be a phenomenal year for all of us. So thank you very much for the time and the opportunity. Thank you.

# Wormhole Labs unveils 'Sunrise' gateway to bring MON and other assets to Solana

By [Zack Abrams](https://www.theblock.co/author/zack-abrams)

[Crypto Ecosystems](https://www.theblock.co/category/crypto-ecosystems)
• November 23, 2025, 10:01AM EST

UPDATED: November 23, 2025, 10:09AM EST

Share

Partner offers

The Block may may earn a commission if you use our partner offers, at no extra cost to you.

Start trading cryptoGet a crypto rewards cardGet a crypto loan

#### Quick Take

- Wormhole Labs has launched Sunrise, a liquidity gateway designed to be the “canonical route” for bringing external assets to Solana.
- The platform is launching with immediate support for MON, the native token of the anticipated Monad blockchain, which will go live tomorrow.
- The initiative relies on Wormhole’s Native Token Transfers (NTT) framework to unify liquidity across Solana DEXs like Jupiter and block explorer Orb.

Wormhole Labs, the firm behind the Wormhole cross-chain protocol, today announced the public launch of Sunrise, a new liquidity gateway focused exclusively on the Solana ecosystem.

The product is pitched as a "canonical route" for external assets to enter Solana with day-one liquidity, giving users a single interface to move tokens from their origin chain onto Solana and immediately tap the network’s DeFi venues, according to materials shared with The Block.

According to the announcement, [Sunrise](http://sunrisebridge.xyz) aims to solve the "fragmented" liquidity issues often associated with bridging new tokens, ensuring that new onchain assets are tradable across Solana venues including decentralized exchange Jupiter and supported on Helius-incubated block explorer Orb from day one. Sunrise will launch with day-one support for Monad's [high-profile MON token](https://www.theblock.co/post/380040/monad-token-sale-defies-fizzle-fears-will-end-oversubscribed-on-coinbase), scheduled to begin trading tomorrow, as its first major test case.

"Solana’s vision for internet capital markets means being the platform on which users can engage with any asset, including crypto assets that aren’t originated on Solana," said Kuleen Nimkar, growth lead at the Solana Foundation. "Products like Sunrise are a critical part of enabling this future by giving non-native new assets a seamless, high-liquidity path into the network from day one."

The move comes as interoperability protocols race to capture flows between high-throughput chains.

tags.impact_reason: Scaramucci's book and predictions regarding institutional adoption and regulatory clarity for RWA tokenization by 2026 provide a moderate boost to Solana's potential, although the direct impact is still speculative.

The massive dam represents the old-world financial institutions (BlackRock, JPMorgan), and the glowing data stream pouring through represents Solana and tokenized assets being brought on-chain. The 'Clarity Act' is the unseen mechanism opening the gate. I included the text '2026' because it's the specific year Scaramucci predicts for this breakthrough, making it a high-impact, forward-looking statement stenciled diegetically onto the structure.

The year '2026' is stenciled in a massive, weathered, industrial font on the face of the dam, catching the otherworldly glow from the breach. The background is deep and dark, emphasizing the monumental scale and dramatic tension of this institutional breakthrough.

editorial.headline: Scaramucci Bets on 'Clarity Act' to Unlock Institutional Floodgates in 2026
editorial.standfirst: SkyBridge founder positions new book as the 'informational bridge' for boomers while predicting a regulatory breakthrough that brings BlackRock and JPMorgan on-chain.
key_takeaways: 2026 Breakthrough: Scaramucci predicts the passage of the 'Clarity Act' (H.R. 3633) will clear the path for tokenized real-world assets.
The $4 Trillion Thesis: SkyBridge estimates $4 trillion is spent annually on transaction verification—costs Solana can eliminate.
Institutional Onboarding: New book 'Solana Rising' targets the capital-rich 60+ demographic still skeptical of blockchain utility.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Anthony Scaramucci didn’t come to Abu Dhabi just to sell books; he came to sell a bridge. Speaking at Breakpoint 2025, the SkyBridge Capital founder positioned his newly released title, *Solana Rising*, not merely as literature, but as a strategic tool designed to onboard the "non-believers"—specifically the capital-rich demographic over 60. With institutional resistance still palpable, Scaramucci argues that the gap isn't technological, but generational. To bridge it, he is betting on a narrative shift that frames Solana not as a casino, but as a deflationary force against the **$4 trillion** spent globally on transaction verification costs.

However, the real alpha lies in his regulatory outlook. Scaramucci explicitly pegged 2026 as the year of the "Clarity Act"—a reference to the **Digital Asset Market Clarity Act (H.R. 3633)** currently navigating legislative gridlock. Despite acknowledging lingering Democratic opposition, Scaramucci views its passage next year as a near-certainty. This legislation is the missing link for firms like **BlackRock**, **Blackstone**, and **JPMorgan**, providing the legal certainty required to move from experimental pilots to mainnet tokenization of real-world assets.

The implications of this regulatory unlock are massive. Scaramucci posits that once the legal framework aligns with the technological capability, the transaction cost reduction will drive an inevitable migration of traditional finance to the "operating layer" of Solana. While the current market focuses on price action, SkyBridge’s thesis is grounded in operational efficiency: eliminating the friction of credit card fees, wire transfers, and legal verification is a multi-trillion-dollar opportunity that legacy finance can no longer ignore.

Looking ahead, the message to the Breakpoint audience was clear: the infrastructure is ready, but the sales pitch needs to mature. By equipping the "believers" with a codified argument for the "non-believers," Scaramucci is preparing for a 2026 where Solana isn't just rising in price, but integrating into the plumbing of the global economy. If his prediction holds, the next twelve months will see the transition from speculative interest to institutional deployment.
